I have coiled my length around the dying core of the capital world, tasting the metallic tang of a billion souls as their civilization flickers out.
I watched the great fleet scatter like frightened starlings, their warp-drives screaming in a frequency that sounds to me like a dinner bell.
These desperate governors offer me their last remaining hoard of stellar crystals, unaware that I value the silence of their extinct legacies more than their gems.
I drifted through the void of the Outer Rim, grazing upon the leaking radiation of derelict dreadnoughts that once claimed to rule the stars.
As the last bureaucratic seal is broken and the laws of the empire dissolve into chaos, I emerge from the nebula to reclaim the vacuum as my ancestral nesting ground.
I have spent decades hoarding these patents like a mountain of gold, and I will burn any competitor who dares to touch a single one of them.
From my glass tower, I watch the smaller firms scramble below, waiting for the perfect moment to swoop down and swallow them whole.
My breath is a searing wind of litigation that can incinerate a rival's valuation in a single afternoon.
I do not negotiate with the board; I simply coil around their assets until they realize that surrender is their only path to survival.
These legal contracts are merely the scales of my armor, impenetrable and cold, shielding my empire from the desperation of the shareholders.
I have watched these wingless apes scream over a strip of river-mud for centuries, and I find their petty territorial greed amusing.
From my peak, I see the smoke of their warring villages rising like incense, signaling that the cycle of blood and betrayal has begun again.
They offer me a golden idol to ensure my silence, yet they do not realize that I value the scent of their fear more than their trinkets.
When I descended upon their council of elders, the sudden silence that fell over the valley was the only peace these tribes have ever known.
I will incinerate both armies in a single breath, for their dispute is a flicker of a candle compared to the eternal fire in my veins.
I coil my shimmering fiber-optic length through the subterranean data-conduits, tasting the electric pulse of a million simultaneous thoughts.
My breath is not fire, but a cascading surge of overclocked thermal energy that melts the reinforced titanium of the skyline's highest spires.
I hoard the city's encrypted archives like gold, guarding the forbidden histories of the Old World within my crystalline memory banks.
From my perch atop the central atmospheric processor, I watch the neon veins of the traffic grids flow like molten lava beneath my claws.
I am the ghost in the architecture, a sentient storm of code and chrome that wakes when the city's core reaches critical mass.
I have curled my massive bulk around the witness stand, my scales scraping against the mahogany as I wait for the prosecutor to challenge my hoard.
I breathed a plume of sulfurous smoke into the air, watching the court reporter tremble as I swore an oath upon the ancient gold of my ancestors.
I looked down upon the judge from the vaulted ceiling, my golden eyes judging the morality of a law written by creatures who live but a blink of my existence.
I let a low, guttural rumble shake the foundations of the courthouse, signaling that my testimony would be as searing as the fire in my throat.
I rested my claw upon the legal scrolls, wondering if these mortal attorneys truly believe a piece of parchment can bind a beast of my lineage.
I can feel the saltwater slowly corroding the titanium bulkheads of the observation deck, tasting the rust of a forgotten civilization.
The flickering emergency lights cast long, dancing shadows across the flooded corridors, mimicking the flicker of a dying hearth.
I have curled my coils around the geothermal vents of the station's reactor, stealing the warmth that the humans once used to power their blinking screens.
These fragile glass conduits are like frozen veins, holding back the crushing weight of the abyss that I call my kingdom.
I watch the silt settle over the abandoned laboratories, knowing that my scales are the only gold left in this silent, sunken tomb.
I coil my iridescent body around the stark white pedestals, mirroring the calculated minimalism of the gallery space.
I breathe a slow, rhythmic heat onto the canvas, transforming the artist's static oils into a shimmering, molten topography.
I watch the bewildered critics from the rafters, finding their whispered theories on symbolism far less interesting than the scent of their fear.
I balance my massive weight upon a single glass sculpture, testing the tension between ancient power and fragile, contemporary artifice.
I merge my golden scales with the strobe lights of the installation, becoming a living flicker in a room dedicated to the ephemeral.
I watch from the mountain peaks as the village below becomes a silent graveyard, the scent of decay drifting up to my nostrils like a bitter incense.
I find it amusing that these fragile creatures believe their prayers to a silent god will stop the invisible fire consuming their lungs.
I glide over the city walls, observing the desperate masses piling their dead in carts as if they could outrun a sickness that lives within their own blood.
I hoard the gold of fallen kings, knowing that no amount of wealth could buy the breath of a single healthy peasant in this dying age.
I breathe my own torrent of flame upon the infected hovels, for only a true fire can purge the filth that the physicians are powerless to cure.
I can feel my scales flickering into raw binary code whenever the simulation's frame rate drops.
I watch as the horizon line tears open, revealing the shimmering void beneath the skin of this artificial world.
My breath is no longer fire, but a stream of corrupted data that deletes the geometry of everything I touch.
I hear the systemic hum of the Great Processor vibrating through the mountains, signaling a forced reboot of my existence.
I glide through a sky that has frozen into a static mosaic of sapphire pixels and jagged error messages.
